subject: Re: warp crashes on motion correction
body:
Hi, thanks for the quick reply,

I was running 1.06 and was crashing. Updated to 1.07 beta and everything 
works well (really well actually :))

Cheers

Rafa

On Thursday, 18 April 2019 12:47:13 UTC+2, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>
> Hi Rafa,
>
> was that with 1.0.6 or 1.0.7? Using 1.0.7, the movie goes through all 
> steps (including BoxNet, which leaves 3.7 GB for everything else) without 
> crashes on a 1080 with 8 GB. If it's 1.0.7 and it crashes specifically at 
> the motion estimation step, reducing the upper frequency in the settings 
> should help. I used 0.4 Ny.
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
> On Thursday, April 18, 2019 at 11:52:11 AM UTC+2, r.ferna...@gmail.com 
> <javascript:> wrote:
>>
>> Hi,
>>
>> Warp is crashing without any feedback/error message when I try to correct 
>> motion on a Falcon3 movie (75 frames). I tried with different settings but 
>> it crashes the same way in all
>>
>> I can see the memory used by GPU gets to 6.5GB and stays like that until 
>> it crashes (first empties GPU then warp closes). So it doesn't seem to be 
>> filling up the GPU...
>>
>> Latest WARP version running on win10
>> 2x2070ti (8GB)
>>
>> I have sent you a link to a movie for you to be able to reproduce the 
>> problem
>>
>> Thx!
>>
>> Rafa
>>
>
